text:
It芒聙聶s Day 8 of the Olympics, and we芒聙聶ve got a big one on tap. For many out there, you芒聙聶ve now made it back to the safe refuge of the weekend and will have an easier time watching. (Could that be why the schedule makers chose Saturday and Sunday to give us the only two days in which track, swimming and gymnastics all overlap? Maybe!) My daily guide is once again here to help everyone know what to watch.

You can also subscribe toSports Illustrated芒聙聶s Daily Rings, my daily podcast where Dan Gartland and I go into significantly more detail on every sport every night. Our regular listeners were 100% ready for exciting moments in judo and trampoline Friday, and you still have time to join us for the rest of the Games. New episodes have been published every night before NBC芒聙聶s prime-time coverage is even over.

Here芒聙聶s what I芒聙聶ll be watching Saturday, August 3.

This is not a comprehensive list;visit Olympics.comfor a full schedule. All events will stream live on Peacock. I芒聙聶ll also be tweeting out a schedule of medal events, Team USA games and other favorites each day.

My Day 8 Olympics viewing guide!Track, swimming and gymnastics in one day. (And kayak cross!)#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/PQkO1NgOuh

Before we get to those typically marquee sports, I want to start out with something a little different. Kayak cross is a new event in the Olympics this year, and I think it芒聙聶s about to be a breakout hit.

You may have seen some canoe slalom races already at these Olympics芒聙聰the events where canoes (or kayaks, they race both) are in the water with rapids and competitors are trying to paddle through and around gates as they take turns in a timed event. For the first time in an Olympics, we will now see multiple boats in the water at once, racing each other to the finish line.脗

Friday during the time trials, they were on the course solo. Saturday and Sunday we芒聙聶ll get the real thing. Saturday will start with heats of three riders per race and the finals will have four in the water at the same time..

Did I mention the kayaks start out elevated above the course on a ramp that opens at the starting gate and dumps them into the water? Or that the riders have to do a barrel roll at some point during the race? Admit it: A part of you thinks I芒聙聶m making this up as a prank. But truly, they have to dunk themselves, come back up for air and paddle to the finish line. I芒聙聶m telling you; everyone will be talking about this over the weekend. So you should check it out.

Round 1 for the women starts at 9:30 a.m. ET, and the men will start at 10:40 a.m. ET. Team USA芒聙聶s Evy Leibfarth, who medaled earlier in these Olympics, has qualified in kayak cross as well.

The action on the track is heating up, and among the five medal events Saturday are a couple of big ones.

Let芒聙聶s start with the women芒聙聶s 100 meters, where Team USA芒聙聶s Sha芒聙聶Carri Richardson is the favorite to win gold. She芒聙聶s been one of the faces of the U.S. delegation going into these Olympics, and this will be a huge moment for her.

In the shot put, AmericanRyan Crouser is going for his third straight Olympic gold, while his top competition may be fellow American Joe Kovacs 芒聙娄 who took silver to Crouser in Rio and Tokyo.

The other finals we芒聙聶ll see are the women芒聙聶s triple jump, the 4X400 mixed relay (in which Team USAset a world record during the heats) and the men芒聙聶s decathlon. The full session of track and field starts at 1:35 p.m. ET.

With the team and individual all-arounds behind us, gymnastics will begin handing out medals on individual apparatuses. On Saturday we芒聙聶ll see the living legend, America芒聙聶s favorite gymnast, an all-time great at their craft. Of course, I芒聙聶m talking about pommel horse specialist Stephen Nedoroscik, who will try to follow up hismoment of glory in the team competitionwith an individual medal.

Oh right, we芒聙聶ll see Simone Biles, too. She and Jade Carey will compete in the vault, beginning at 10:20 a.m. ET. Biles is already themost decorated gymnast in history, now a two-time Olympic all-around champion, and she芒聙聶ll have a few more opportunities to add to her medal count.

Swimming is still going strong, and Saturday芒聙聶s 2:30 p.m. ET session includes four finals: the men芒聙聶s 100-meter fly, women芒聙聶s 200-meter IM, women芒聙聶s 800-meter free and the mixed 4X100 medley relay. The women芒聙聶s 800 will be another showdown between Katie Ledecky and Australia芒聙聶s Ariarne Titmus, which is must-see TV.

The U.S. women芒聙聶s soccer team has reached the win-or-go-home phase, and has a crucial quarterfinal against Japan at 9 a.m. ET. The team needs to win two more matches for a medal and three for a gold one.

Surfing has been delayed by poor weather, but the finals are finally scheduled to really, actually happen. They are slated for 3:24 p.m. ET, but check the schedule again Saturday just in case.

If you missed the experience of seeing French judo legendTeddy Riner compete in frontof his home crowd Friday, you芒聙聶ll get another chance in the team event Saturday. France is the defending Olympic champion, which is how he got one of his six medals.脗

The U.S. women芒聙聶s 3x3 teamhas picked it up after a slow start. They will play against China at 1:05 p.m. ET and then possibly a second game depending on the result.

The U.S. men芒聙聶s five-on-five basketball team is also back in action, against Puerto Rico at 11:15 a.m. ET.

The U.S. men芒聙聶s water polo team has a crucial game against Montenegro, with both teams fighting to advance out of group play. That match gets underway at 10:35 a.m. ET.

Mitch Goldich is a senior editor for Sports Illustrated, mostly focused on the NFL. He has also covered the Olympics extensively and written on a variety of sports since joining SI in 2014. His work has been published by The New York Times, Baseball Prospectus and Food & Wine, among other outlets. Goldich has a bachelor芒聙聶s in journalism from Lehigh University and a master芒聙聶s in journalism from the Medill School at Northwestern University. He is a Philly native, now living in Boston, and he invented and popularized the octopus, when the same player scores a touchdown and the ensuing two-point conversion.

title: Kayak Cross Is About to Become the Breakout Sport of the Paris Olympics
description: The event is new at the Games this year and it will feature plenty of barrel-rolling action on the water. Plus, Stephen Nedoroscik and Simone Biles compete for more medals.
